model_output_raw_text: {"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-4","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = True","new":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = False","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The prior cosine schedule under this short budget degraded results; switch back to constant LR and raise Adam step size to improve early progress within 256 updates.","secondary_modes":["summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 8","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Increase representational capacity of the micro-resnet backbone with a minimal single-constant change; this often improves CIFAR-10 loss even in short fixed-step training.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 24","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The previous capacity increase helped substantially; a further moderate width increase is the most direct continuation to reduce underfitting within the fixed 256-step budget.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 24","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"insert_after","function":"","old":"","new":"","anchor":"            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),","text":"\n            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Width 24 overfit/under-optimized under fixed steps; reverting to 16 and adding one more residual block increases depth at a better compute/optimization tradeoff.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 8e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"With the added residual depth, a slightly lower Adam learning rate can improve optimization stability and validation loss within the same 256-step budget.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 8e-4","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The reduced learning rate hurt loss; restore 1e-3, which matched the current best-performing configuration for this deeper 2-block network.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"AdamW often improves generalization versus Adam at similar step budgets by decoupling weight decay, making it a high-value optimizer-family test with minimal code change.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"With AdamW and increased depth, stronger decoupled weight decay is a low-complexity regularization tweak that can improve validation loss under short training.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 64","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"In fixed-step evaluation, increasing batch size exposes the model to more unique training examples within the same 256 updates, which can lower validation loss for this stable AdamW setup.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"After the large batch-size increase, slightly reducing weight decay may improve fit and lower validation loss while keeping the strong AdamW + deeper-block configuration unchanged.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.2e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"With batch size 128 and AdamW, a modest LR increase can improve optimization speed in the fixed 256-step budget and may further reduce validation loss.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.2e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.4e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Performance improved when moving from 1e-3 to 1.2e-3, so a continued modest LR increase is a high-probability next step for this fixed-step AdamW regime.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.4e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.2e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"1.4e-3 overstepped and degraded loss; revert to 1.2e-3, which currently gives the best verified result.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = False","new":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = True","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"Now that LR and batch are tuned high, adding cosine decay over the fixed 256 steps can improve end-of-training refinement and potentially reduce validation loss further.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = True","new":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = False","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"Cosine decay hurt this configuration; revert to constant LR, which produced the current best result.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 32","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Increase classifier head capacity with a single lightweight change to better exploit stronger features from the deeper residual trunk at the same training budget.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 20","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"After improving the classifier head, a moderate trunk-width increase is a targeted next capacity bump that may improve feature quality without the larger regression seen at 24 channels.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 20","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"20 channels slightly regressed and increased runtime substantially; revert to 16-channel trunk, which is the best verified width so far in this budget.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.2e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.1e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"With the larger FC head now in place, a slight LR reduction may improve stability and final loss while preserving the strong high-batch AdamW regime.","secondary_modes":["layout"]}
